EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A northerly swell produced life-threatening rip currents along the northern beaches of Puerto Rico from late December into early 2026.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 53-year-old man drowned at Ocean Park Beach after being pulled by a rip current while attempting to assist a family member in the water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Fresh to locally strong east to northeast winds and a long-period northwesterly swell, resulted in a high risk of rip currents along northern beaches of Puerto Rico, and Culebra. Two 21 year old males were swept away by currents in Caracoles Beach, Arecibo. One was rescued with injuries and the other was found deceased.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two 21-year-old males from Corozal were swept away by rip currents at Caracoles Beach along Highway PR-681 in Arecibo. One individual was rescued by the US Coast Guard and transported to a hospital. The body of the second individual was recovered the following day, Jan 19th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broad surface high pressure over the Central Atlantic resulted in breezy conditions on January 21st with gusts up to 35 mph reported over coastal areas.  Brief, strong winds not associated with a thunderstorm were reported to affect Santa Elena neighborhood in Guayanilla that afternoon. Residential solar panels sustained damage, and several trash cans were displaced. A resident reported loud wind noise at the time of the event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report of brief, strong winds not associated with a thunderstorm was received. |Residential solar panels sustained damage, and several trash cans were displaced in the Santa Elena neighborhood in Guayanilla. A resident reported loud wind noise at the time of the event.
